author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
hari he!
arthera sañcaye, viṣaya⋅prayāse,
āna kathā prajalpane
āna adhikāra, niyama⋅āgrahe,
asatsaṅga⋅saŋghaṭane

asthira siddhānte, rahinu majiyā,
haribhakti raila dūre
e hṛdaye mātra, parahiṁsā⋅mada,
pratiṣṭhā, śaṭhatā, sphure

e saba āgraha, chāḍite nārinu,
āpana doṣete mari’
janama viphala, hoila āmāra,
ekhana ki kari’ hari

āmi ta’ patita, patita⋅pāvana,
tomāra pavitra nāma
se⋅sambandha dhari’, tomāra caraṇe,
śaraṇa loinu hāma
translation_intro: From The Harmonist, April 1929. Edited by Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati.
en_translation:
Hari! Gathering wealth, Striving after things of this world, Idle talk on topics of the hour, Misunderstanding the sphere of my duties, Adherence to the letter of the law, Contriving to mix with worldly people,

Uncertainty of principle, - Continue to enamour me still. Devotion to Hari is afar off. This heart only breeds Malice against others, pride, Desire for fame and cunning,

All these inclinations I could not leave. I am ruined by my own fault, This birth of mine Is rendered useless Hari! what now I am to do?

I am fallen, indeed, Thy Holy Name Is Saviour of those gone astray. By this relationship At Thy feet I take Refuge.
